top_signals.title: Malaysia May Again Host Formula One Racing as Iran War Puts Bahrain in Doubt
top_signals.summary: Malaysia could host a Formula One race for the first time in nine years, potentially replacing the Bahrain Grand Prix as the Middle East conflict drags on, according to the country’s Youth and Sports Minister Mohammed Taufiq Johari.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Global LNG Giants Target Cambodia as New Energy Market
top_signals.summary: Some of the world’s biggest heavyweights in liquefied natural gas exports are interested in adding Cambodia to their client list as the Southeast Asian nation build sits first LNG-fueled power station. The United States, Australia, Canada, and some Southeast Asian countries are among those interested in supplying fuel for the new power plant, Cambodia’s energy minister, Keo Rottanak, told Bloomberg in an interview. Cambodia gets 63% of its electricity from renewable sources, according to Bloomberg, but according to the International…
